ddls:
CREATE TABLE "qos_policies" (
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each QoS policy */,
  "policy_name" TEXT /* Name of the QoS policy */,
  "policy_description" TEXT /* Description of the policy, detailing its purpose and application */,
  "policy_category" TEXT /* Category of the policy (e.g., Reliability, Priority, Latency) */,
  "policy_usage" TEXT /* Where the policy is typically applied (e.g., Network, Application) */,
  "default_value_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the default value for the policy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "policy_values" (
  "value_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each policy value */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the QoS policy this value is linked to */,
  "value" TEXT /* Actual value of the policy (e.g., RELIABLE, BEST_EFFORT) */,
  "meaning" TEXT /* Explanation of what the value means */,
  "concerns" TEXT /* Concerns related to this value in implementation */,
  "rxo" TEXT /* RxO (Read/Write) status or behaviors */,
  "changeable" INTEGER /* Indicates if the value can be changed dynamically */,
  "min_value" INTEGER /* Minimum value for the policy setting */,
  "max_value" INTEGER /* Maximum value for the policy setting */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the policy value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("value_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policy_values_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_id") REFERENCES qos_policies ("policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "max_blocking_time_settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "max_blocking_time_description" TEXT /* Description of the max blocking time */,
  "default_value" INTEGER /* Default maximum blocking time in milliseconds */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the QoS policy this setting is linked to */,
  "is_dynamic" INTEGER /* Indicate if the setting can be changed dynamically */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last modification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_max_blocking_time_settings_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_id") REFERENCES qos_policies ("policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transport_priorities" (
  "priority_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each priority setting */,
  "priority_value" INTEGER /* Integer value representing the priority */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of what the priority means */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the QoS policy this setting is linked to */,
  "is_dynamic" INTEGER /* Indicate if the setting can be changed dynamically */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last modification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("priority_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_transport_priorities_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_id") REFERENCES qos_policies ("policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network condition entry */,
  "condition_description" TEXT /* Description of the network condition (e.g., high latency) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the condition was recorded */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the QoS policy this condition affects */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the network condition */,
  "resolution_status" TEXT /* Status of the condition resolution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "policy_analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics entry */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the QoS policy related to this metric */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the metric recording */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric collected */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the metric collected */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the service type */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of metric (e.g., Performance, Reliability) */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for the metric */,
  "is_alert" INTEGER /* Indicate if the metric value exceeds the threshold */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policy_analytics_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES service_types ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policy_analytics_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_id") REFERENCES qos_policies ("policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the user role (e.g., Admin, Network Engineer) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with this role in the system */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for system login */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for authentication */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user role */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who made the change */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the change made */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the change was made */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the change was made */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action (e.g., Create, Update, Delete) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the change (e.g., Success, Failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_types" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service type */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service (e.g., Video Streaming) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the service and its QoS needs */,
  "service_category" TEXT /* Category of the service (e.g., Real-time, Batch) */,
  "service_provider" TEXT /* Provider of the service */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the service type was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_qos" (
  "service_qos_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the service-QoS association */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the service type */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the QoS policy applied to the service */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Indicate if this is the default QoS setting for the service */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the association was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_qos_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_qos_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_id") REFERENCES qos_policies ("policy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_qos_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES service_types ("service_id")
)
